mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka on Kubernetes or OpenShift. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kafka clusters, making it suitable for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Strimzi Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Strimzi Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts

```

> This is the official Helm chart repository for Strimzi.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are required for the Strimzi Kafka Operator to function correctly. This step installs them.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://strimzi.io/install/latest/strimzi-cluster-operator.yaml

```

> This installs the necessary CRDs for Strimzi.

steps.title: Install Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator using Helm in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --create-namespace --version 0.44.0

```

> Replace 0.44.0 with the desired version.

steps.title: Create Kafka Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster using a Custom Resource definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kafka.strimzi.io/v1beta2
kind: Kafka
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: kafka
spec:
  kafka:
    replicas: 3
    listeners:
      - name: plain
        port: 9092
        type: internal
        tls: false
      - name: tls
        port: 9093
        type: internal
        tls: true
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
  zookeeper:
    replicas: 3
    storage:
      type: ephemeral
EOF

```

> This creates a Kafka cluster named 'my-cluster'.

steps.title: Verify Kafka Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Kafka cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> You should see 'my-cluster' with a status of 'Ready'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete your Kafka cluster and all data.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka

kubectl delete namespace kafka

```

> This command removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your Kafka data. This step upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain.
> Replace 0.44.0 with the desired version.
> Verify that the Kafka cluster is still running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kafka cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka cluster may not have enough resources allocated or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and configurations in your Kafka Custom Resource.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Zookeeper'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Zookeeper may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Verify the Zookeeper pods are running with 'kubectl get pods -n kafka'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No such host'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka service may not be properly exposed.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure it is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The storage class may not have enough capacity.

**Fix:**
Check the storage class and ensure it has sufficient resources.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the 'kafka.spec.storage.type' field for certain configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.43.0, ensure to update your Kafka Custom Resource to match the new storage configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka' and its status should be 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
